REPLY TO DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX COSTS FILED BY PLAINTIFF WILLIAMS, KEVIN

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KEVIN WILLIAMS' MOTION TO STRIKE OR ALTERNATIVELY TAX COSTS - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. CCP 1032 STATES THAT COSTS ARE AVAILABLE AS A MATTER OF RIGHT WHEN THE PREVAILING PARTY IS WITHIN ONE OF THE FOUR CATEGORIES DESIGNATED BY STATUTE. ( 1032, SUBDS. (A)(4), (B).) 1032 INCLUDES FOUR CATEGORIES OF PARTIES: (1) THE PARTY WITH A NET MONETARY RECOVERY, (2) THE DEFENDANT IN WHOSE FAVOR A DISMISSAL WAS ENTERED, (3) THE DEFENDANT WHERE NEITHER PLAINTIFF NOR DEFENDANT RECOVERS ANY RELIEF, AND (4) THE DEFENDANT AGAINST WHOM PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RECOVERED ANY RELIEF. (SEE MICHELL V. OLICK (1996) 49 CAL. APP. 4TH 1194, 1197-98.) HERE, DISMISSAL WAS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANTSON MARCH 20, 2015, THEREFORE DEFENDANTS ARE WITHIN CCP 1032. 1033.5, SUBDIVISION (A), LISTS SPECIFIC ITEMS OF COSTS WHICH MAY BE RECOVERED AS A MATTER OF RIGHT PURSUANT TO SECTION 1032. AMONG ALLOWABLE COSTS ARE FILING FEES, JURY FEES AND JUROR FOOD, DEPOSITION EXPENSES, SERVICE OF PROCESS COSTS, WITNESS FEES, TRANSCRIPTS ORDERED BY THE COURT, AND EXPENSES IN PREPARING BLOWUPS AND PHOTOCOPIES OF EXHIBITS. HERE, DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FILING FEES AND DEPOSITION COSTS. THESE FALL UNDER 1033.5, SUBDIVISION (A), THEREFORE DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO RECOVER THESE COSTS AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS TO THESE COSTS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO RECOVER COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH ARBITRATION. THE REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DECISION IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Alternatively Tax Costs
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - KEVIN WILLIAMS, TEL. NO. 1-415-424-8221, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. ADAN KOSS OF MURPHY PEARSON & FEENEY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KEVIN WILLIAMS' Motion To Strike Or Alternatively Tax Costs - Plaintiff's motion to strike costs is granted in part. CCP 1032 states that costs are available as a matter of right when the prevailing party is within one of the four categories designated by statute. ( 1032, subds. (a)(4), (b).) 1032 includes four categories of parties: (1) the party with a net monetary recovery, (2) the defendant in whose favor a dismissal was entered, (3) the defendant where neither plaintiff nor defendant recovers any relief, and (4) the defendant against whom plaintiff has not recovered any relief. (See Michell v. Olick (1996) 49 Cal. App. 4th 1194, 1197-98.) Here, dismissal was entered in favor of Defendantson March 20, 2015, therefore Defendants are within CCP 1032. 1033.5, subdivision (a), lists specific items of costs which may be recovered as a matter of right pursuant to section 1032. Among allowable costs are filing fees, jury fees and juror food, deposition expenses, service of process costs, witness fees, transcripts ordered by the court, and expenses in preparing blowups and photocopies of exhibits. Here, Defendants' request filing fees and deposition costs. These fall under 1033.5, subdivision (a), therefore Defendants are entitled to recover these costs and Plaintiff's motion as to these costs is denied. The Court finds Defendants are entitled to recover costs associated with arbitration. The request for statement of decision is taken under submission. The court to look at the proposed order submitted by the defendants. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Or Relief From Arbitration Due To Indigency
Off Calendar Jan-20-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kevin Williams, plaintiff / moving party appearing in pro per (415-424-8221)
Adam M. Koss, Esq., of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (415-788-1900), appearing for / opposing party Eric Safire

RULING:
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration or relief from arbitration due to indigency is denied provided that" 1) defendants' "advance" of the arbitration fees is not disclosed to the arbitrator until the arbitrator has rendered a decision on the substantive issues and 2) defendants' "advance" of the arbitration fees does not preclude the arbitrator from determining that defendant should bear all or more than 50% of the arbitration fees and thus plaintiff is not required to "reimburse" defendants for all or some of the arbitration fees "advanced" by defendants. Counsel are to meet and confer to select an arbitrator if Judge Jeffrey Ross is not available. No later than the end of the day on 2/16 each side will provide 3 names and any available information such as C.V.s to the other side. By 2/23 each side will strike two names from the list of the other side, then meet and confer to decide on the arbitrator out of the two names. If the parties cannot reach an agreement then submit the two names and their CVs to the Court. On 3/2 the Court will select one as the arbitrator. Mr. Koss is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
